name: Christmas Ornament Cookies- Country Living
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, beat butter and shortening on medium speed until well blended. Add sugar and beat until light. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
2. In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually add the flour mixture to the butter mixture, beating until a soft dough forms. Shape dough into 3 equal balls and wrap in plastic; refrigerate at least 30 minutes.
3. To cut and bake cookies: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease two baking sheets lightly. Line each sheet with waxed paper. Roll out one ball of dough on waxed paper between papers to 1/8-inch thickness. Remove top piece of waxed paper. Using a 7-inch bauble-shaped cookie cutter, cut as many shapes as possible from the dough, leaving at least 1/2 inch between each shape. Gather trimmings and roll them out again.
4. Invert the waxed paper with the baubles onto the greased baking sheet and peel off the waxed paper. Repeat rolling, cutting, and inverting baubles to fill the second baking sheet. If making cookies for ornaments, use a toothpick to pierce a small hole about 1/2 inch from the top of each cookie.
5. Bake cookies 8 to 10 minutes or until golden. Let cool on the sheets for 5 minutes before transferring to wire racks to cool completely. Repeat rolling, cutting, and baking with remaining dough balls and trimmings.
6. To decorate: Prepare Royal Icing as directed. Divide icing in half; place one half in a medium bowl. Divide the other half into three small bowls or cups. Tint one bowl pale yellow using yellow food coloring (see Note). Mix small amounts of icing with burgundy, teal, and violet food coloring.
7. Pipe the yellow icing onto each cookie using a small pastry bag fitted with a writing tip; avoid covering the pierced hole. Let dry completely before adding additional decorations. Pipe decorative lines, dots, and curls on the front of cookies using the colored icings.
8. Store cookies in an airtight container.
ROYAL ICING: In a large bowl, beat together two 1-pound packages of confectioners' sugar, six large egg whites (or meringue powder with cream of tartar as directed), and 1 teaspoon cream of tartar until combined. Increase speed to high and beat until thick and fluffy, about 6 minutes. Cover tightly and store until ready for use.
Note: Wilton's Egg Yellow, Burgundy, Teal, and Grape Violet food coloring and meringue powder can be purchased at cake decorating supply stores or by calling Wilton Industries at (800) 772-7771.
